content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcel PnecrrcEs CouvussroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-s660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
October 14,2003
Charles J. Wright
5418 State Avenue
Sacramento, CA 95819
Re:
Your Request for fnformal Assistance
Our FiIe No. I-03-123
DearMr. Wright:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 'Act').1 Sirrc" yo-ur inquiry is ge,neral
in nature, we are treating your request as one for informal assistancl.2 6eg. tSfZ% subd.
ox8)(c).)
QUESTION
Are there restrictions on your participation or is there any conflict of interest in
your participation as a board member on one of the state's Alta Regional boards?
CONCLUSION
No.
FACTS
You have been nominated and have tentatively accepted a position as a board
member on one of California's 13 area regional centers which were established by the
state Lanterman Developmental Disabilities Services Act. The specific board in question
is the Alta california Regional center located in Sacramento.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations apperat Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Pursuant to regulation lS32g,subdivision (c)(3)(copy enclosed), informal assistance does not
confer immunity.


File No. I-03-123
Page No. 2
The mission statement for Alta California Regional Center ("AltaRegional,), a
pivate nonprofit corporation, is "to assist persons with developmental disabiiities, '
including infants at risk, and their families by providin g a16 securing those services and
supports necessary to maximize opporfunities and choices.,,
The stated role of a board member of a regional center is to, among other things,
'lmplement the vision and mission of the regional center to provide quality supports and
services to our consumers." As a board member, you are not compensated for your
patticipation although you are eligible forreimbursement for travel mileage to and from
board meetings and for "atthoized meetings.,,
You and your wife have a four-year old daughter who was bom with
developmental disabilities. She has, since her birth, received seryices from Alta Regional
and continues to receive limited services from Alta Regional although not to the extent
she did during the first two years ofher life.
There are 13 regional centers throughout California which receive state funding.
You are a chief of staff for Senator Betty Karnette, but do not vote as a board member]on
any services directly affecting any consumer receiving seryices from any of the regional
centers.
ANALYSIS
As a designated employee of the Senate, you are required by the Act to report
income and gifts over certain amounts on your annual statement ofeconomic interests.
(S 87302.) Furthermore, as applied to officials in your capacity,the Act limits gifts to
you to $340 per calendar year from certain sources. Finally, section 87100 profrbit, yo,
from participating in decisions as a Senate staff member which will have a reasonably
foreseeable and material financial effect on your economic interests.
As an Alta Regional board mernber, you do not receive a salary. The only funds
you receive from Alta Regional are reimbursement for expenses associated with
attending board meetings. Section 8zo3o, subdivision (bX2), excludes these
reimbursements from the definition of "income." Since you receive no income from Alta
Regional, Alta Regional is not a source of income to you. Furthermore, as a nonprofit,
service on their board will not create any reporting obligations or conflict of interest
problems for you under the Act.3 (connor Aal ir" Lettir,No. A-94-247.)
Receipt by your child of services by Alta Regional implicates the issue of
whether, in providing these services, Alta Regional is making a gift to you personally.
We have previously advised in similar circumstances that sr.h asistance dies not
constitute a gift to the public official. (See Connor Advice Letter, supra.) In the Connor
letter, we advised that the provision of legal services to the daughter of a public official
by a chaitable and educational organization to assist in the defense of civil rights
- . ' Co*p*" section 87103, subdivision (d), conceming an economic interest in a..business entity',
created by serving as a director ofthe business.


File No. I-03-123
Page No. 3
affectrngthe disabled child, were not gifts to the official pursuant to regulation 1g944.
The letter reasoned that the representation was subject to the "rebate or discount,,
exception stated in section 82028. Because the Alta Regional orgaruzation is providing
services to your daughter as part of its mission and policies, which are made available to
the generalpublic, we find the exception applies in this case, as well.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa
General
By:
Tocher
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure
CSTjg

QUESTION: Are there restrictions on your participation or is there any conflict of interest in
your participation as a board member on one of the state's Alta Regional boards?

CONCLUSION: No.
